description:
WHY: The recommended balls deployment is a bare central hub (core.bare=true) with all work in linked worktrees. Bare-ness is load-bearing, not incidental: it's a hard git-level invariant that nobody — agent or human — can commit or even stage on the operating branch from the root. Git itself refuses. This is the strongest possible enforcement of the worktree-only philosophy SKILL.md already preaches ('edits go in the worktree, not on the operating branch'), but the docs never state that the bare hub is how you get that guarantee, or what operating against one looks like.

GAP: No committed doc (README, SKILL, docs/SPEC-*) mentions bare-repository operation at all. Worse, README.md and SKILL.md repeatedly instruct 'run bl close from the repo root' — which is fatal in a bare repo until bl-8cf7 lands. So the docs don't just omit the model; they prescribe a flow that's currently broken there. A new agent or user landing in a bare hub has no documented way to even list tasks, and 'git status' at the root returns 'fatal: this operation must be run in a work tree' with no guidance.

SCOPE:
- Document the bare central-hub model and WHY it's chosen (hard no-direct-commits invariant > convention enforced by a hook).
- 'git status' at a bare root is meaningless/fatal by design; the root's loose files are stale leftovers, not git's work tree. Observation = 'bl list' for tasks + 'git status' inside the active .balls-worktrees/<id>/ worktree.
- State where bl must run and that read-only/root commands work from a bare root once bl-8cf7 lands.
- Reconcile every 'run from the repo root' line in SKILL.md/README.md with bare-repo behavior (depends on bl-8cf7 defining that behavior — hence the dep).

Belongs alongside the README 'state worktree' / glossary section. Depends on bl-8cf7 because the docs must describe the post-fix behavior, not today's broken one.
title: Docs: document the bare central-hub repo model
